tasks.title: Design outflow request intent structure and generalize hub intent creation
tasks.description: Analyze and design the generalized hub request intent structure to support both inflow and outflow request intents
tasks.details: Create wrapper functions for inflow (current) and outflow (new) request intents. For inflow: create_inflow_request_intent() wrapper around existing create_cross_chain_request_intent_entry() using FungibleAssetLimitOrder (no signature required). For outflow: create_outflow_request_intent() using OracleGuardedLimitOrder (requires verifier signature). Extend OracleGuardedLimitOrder struct to add requester_address_connected_chain: Option<address> field to store where solver should send tokens on connected chain. Design ensures tokens are locked on hub for outflow vs connected chain for inflow.
tasks.testStrategy: Create unit tests validating both inflow and outflow intent creation with correct token locking behavior and struct field validation

tasks.title: Define connected_chain_fulfill transaction format for Move/Aptos
tasks.description: Design and document the transaction format for immediate solver transfers on Aptos connected chains
tasks.details: Define transaction format using standard Move transfer functions (primary_fungible_store::deposit()) with intent_id encoded in transaction metadata/script arguments. Document exact transaction layout specification for solvers to follow. Create validation function validate_connected_chain_fulfill() that verifies transaction format (not values - contract doesn't know correct values). Transaction must immediately transfer tokens to requester address with no escrow or verifier involvement. Include intent_id for verifier tracking when querying by transaction hash.
tasks.testStrategy: Test transaction format validation, metadata extraction, and ensure standard transfer functions work with appended intent_id metadata

tasks.title: Define connected_chain_fulfill transaction format for EVM
tasks.description: Design and document the transaction format for immediate solver transfers on EVM connected chains
tasks.details: Define transaction format using standard ERC20 transfer(to, amount) with intent_id appended as metadata in transaction data field (32 bytes after function parameters). Data format: 0xa9059cbb (transfer selector) + to (32 bytes) + amount (32 bytes) + intent_id (32 bytes). ERC20 contract ignores extra intent_id bytes, but they remain verifiable on-chain. Create validation function validateConnectedChainFulfill() in IntentEscrow contract for format verification. No contract changes needed for transfers - uses existing ERC20 standard.
tasks.testStrategy: Test transaction data field parsing, metadata extraction, verify ERC20 transfer ignores extra bytes, and validate transaction format checking

tasks.title: Implement Move outflow request intent creation function
tasks.description: Implement create_outflow_request_intent() function in fa_intent_cross_chain.move for outflow request intents
tasks.details: Create function that uses fa_intent_with_oracle::create_fa_to_fa_intent_with_oracle_requirement() to create OracleGuardedLimitOrder intent. Function withdraws actual tokens from user (locked on hub), stores requester_address_connected_chain, sets revocable=false for cross-chain safety, and requires verifier_public_key parameter. Extends existing cross-chain module while maintaining consistency with current inflow patterns. Returns Object<TradeIntent<FungibleStoreManager, OracleGuardedLimitOrder>>.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for outflow intent creation, token withdrawal verification, oracle requirement setup, and requester address storage

tasks.title: Extend OracleGuardedLimitOrder struct with requester address field
tasks.description: Add requester_address_connected_chain field to OracleGuardedLimitOrder struct in fa_intent_with_oracle.move
tasks.details: Modify OracleGuardedLimitOrder struct to include requester_address_connected_chain: Option<address> field. This field stores the connected chain address where solver should send tokens for outflow intents. Update create_fa_to_fa_intent_with_oracle_requirement() function to accept and store this field. Field is Some(address) for outflow intents, None for regular oracle intents. Update all related functions to handle the new field appropriately.
tasks.testStrategy: Test struct modification, field storage and retrieval, ensure backward compatibility with existing oracle intents, validate outflow intent requires non-None address

tasks.title: Implement inflow request intent wrapper function
tasks.description: Create create_inflow_request_intent() wrapper function for clarity and consistency with new outflow function
tasks.details: Implement wrapper around existing create_cross_chain_request_intent_entry() function in fa_intent_cross_chain.move for naming consistency. This maintains current inflow behavior (FungibleAssetLimitOrder, withdraws 0 tokens, no signature required) while providing clear distinction from outflow intents. Function should have identical parameters and behavior to current implementation but with clearer naming convention.
tasks.testStrategy: Test wrapper function maintains exact same behavior as original, verify parameter passing and return values match current implementation

tasks.title: Add Move transaction format validation functions
tasks.description: Implement validation functions for connected_chain_fulfill transaction format in Move contracts
tasks.details: Add validate_connected_chain_fulfill() view function that validates transaction format for Move/Aptos connected chains. Function should verify transaction includes intent_id in metadata, transaction format matches expected structure (standard transfer with metadata), and all required fields are present. Note: validates format only, not values (contract doesn't know correct values - only verifier does). Can be called off-chain by solver before submitting transaction for pre-flight validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Test format validation logic, verify function correctly identifies valid/invalid transaction formats, test off-chain callable behavior

tasks.title: Add EVM transaction format validation functions
tasks.description: Implement validation functions for connected_chain_fulfill transaction format in EVM contracts
tasks.details: Add validateConnectedChainFulfill() view function to IntentEscrow contract that validates transaction format for EVM connected chains. Function should verify transaction data field includes intent_id metadata (32 bytes after function parameters), transaction format matches expected structure (transfer selector + to + amount + intent_id), and all required fields are present and properly formatted. Returns format validation result for off-chain solver usage.
tasks.testStrategy: Test transaction data field validation, verify correct format detection for valid/invalid transactions, ensure proper byte layout verification

tasks.title: Design verifier transaction validation logic
tasks.description: Design comprehensive verifier validation logic for both Move/Aptos and EVM connected chains
tasks.details: Design verifier system to receive transaction hash from solver (via API), query transaction by hash on connected chain, extract intent_id and transaction parameters from transaction data. For EVM: use eth_getTransactionByHash and decode data field. For Aptos: use Aptos RPC and extract from transaction metadata. Validate all parameters match intent requirements: intent_id matches hub request intent, to/requester address matches intent's requester_address_connected_chain, amount matches desired amount, solver address matches reserved solver. Verify transaction confirmed and transfer completed successfully.
tasks.testStrategy: Test transaction hash querying, parameter extraction for both chains, validation logic against intent requirements, and transaction confirmation verification

tasks.title: Implement verifier approval signature generation
tasks.description: Implement verifier approval signature generation after successful transaction validation
tasks.details: After successful validation of connected_chain_fulfill transaction, implement verifier signing of intent_id to generate approval signature. Signature follows same pattern as existing oracle-guarded intents - verifier signs BCS-encoded intent_id. Solver uses this signature to fulfill hub intent via existing oracle-guarded intent mechanism (finish_fa_receiving_session_with_oracle()). Funds always go to reserved solver address regardless of who calls fulfillment function. Integrate with existing verifier infrastructure and approval workflows.
tasks.testStrategy: Test signature generation process, verify signature compatibility with existing oracle-guarded intent verification, test hub intent fulfillment with generated signature
